steps.title: Put the equation in quadratic form
steps.content: Start with the given equation: $$x(11x+25)+c=4$$ Expanding and moving everything to one side gives $$11x^2+25x+(c-4)=0.$$

steps.title: Match the given radical
steps.content: For $11x^2+25x+(c-4)=0$, the quadratic formula has denominator $2(11)=22$, matching the given solution. The expression under the square root must be $185$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Set the discriminant equal to $185$: $$25^2-4(11)(c-4)=185$$ $$801-44c=185$$ $$c=14$$ The result matches.
